summary: A small two-story rectangular house with a raised stonebrick foundation border, oak plank walls with vertical log corner posts, horizontal stonebrick band between floors, many small glass windows in rows, and a dark nether-brick stepped gable roof capped with stonebrick corner/edge blocks.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular two-story box with a centered pitched roof; roof ridge runs along the long axis
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along the roof ridge (longitudinal)

materials.name: brick
materials.role: roof edge/accent (likely stonebrick slabs visible) - ambiguous

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: foundation top/trim (stone slab border around base)

materials.name: fence
materials.role: not observed but possible for balcony/railing (uncertain)

elements: rows of glass windows on front and sides (two rows: one per floor)
stonebrick slab border/foundation platform surrounding base
horizontal stonebrick band between ground and second floor
nether-brick stepped gable roof with stonebrick corner blocks
no visible door on shown facades (entrance not visible from provided angles)
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular footprint ~9x5 blocks (visible roof top occupies inner area with a 1-block stone slab border around).
Raise a 1-block high stonebrick slab border/platform around the base; build oak plank walls two floors tall with a horizontal stonebrick band separating floors.
Place vertical log posts at all four exterior corners spanning both floors; insert rows of glass panes (3 or 4 per side segment) centered on each floor.
Construct a symmetrical nether-brick gable roof along the long axis with stepped layers and stonebrick blocks at the roof corners/ends as caps.
No visible front door in images; entrance likely on an unseen side or omitted — leave opening placement flexible.
uncertainties: Exact footprint block counts (width/depth) estimated from top-down roof image; could be off by ±1-2 blocks.
Material exactness: stone-like trim might be stonebrick or regular stone; roof appears dark (nether_brick) but could be dark oak planks or another dark block.
Presence and placement of an entrance/door is not visible in any provided views.
Interior layout and floor heights are not visible; floor-to-ceiling spacing assumed typical (2-3 blocks per floor).
Some small trim details (slabs vs full blocks at roof edges) are partially occluded and inferred from shadows.
